title: UIState
text: UIState Control whether keyboard accelerators and focus are displayed in selected widgets under Microsoft Windows. UIState = Value Arguments 0 —no behavior changed. Use MS Windows system configuration. (default) 1 —keyboard accelerators are always displayed. 2 —focus indicators are always displayed. 3 —both keyboard accelerators and focus indicators are always displayed. Default value 0 File section [upi] Description On Windows platforms since Windows 2000, the standard behavior is to show no focus indicator on checkboxes or radio groups, nor to underline the keyboard accelerator in menus until you press the Alt key. To underline the keyboard accelerator in all Windows applications, go to Control Panel >Ease of Access Center >Make the keyboard easier to use and select Underline keyboard shortcuts and access keys . To change this behavior only in Uniface applications, use the UIState setting. For example, if you want to have the focus indicator on the checkbox and the radiogroup widget, configure below in your ini file. [UPI] uistate=3 Copy Related Links ucheckbox (CheckBox) The ucheckbox widget enables the user to set a Boolean value in a form component. Uniface provides a logical widget called CheckBox, which is mapped to ucheckbox. uradiogroup (RadioGroup) The uradiogroup widget displays a set of mutually exclusive choices as a group of radio buttons. Uniface provides a logical widget called RadioGroup, which is mapped to uradiogroup. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
